channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: What Is AI Pair Programming? AI Coding Tools for Developers
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology explains AI pair programming as a collaborative approach where AI assists developers in coding tasks. The content highlights how AI enhances debugging, code review, and overall developer productivity through real-world applications.

item.title: FULLY FREE Unlimited API + OpenCode: MiniMax M3,Step 3.7 Flash,Nemotron 3 Ultra,GLM,Kimi!
item.description: AICodeKing: OpenRouter launched Fusion API, a compound model system that sends prompts to multiple models and uses a judge model to select the final answer. The creator tested Fusion against claims of Fable-level performance and found inconsistent results across real-world tasks, concluding it is not a true Fable competitor in current form.

item.title: Why I never trust fully generated Code
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery presents a perspective on the risks and limitations of relying on fully AI-generated code without verification. The content addresses concerns developers should have when using code generation tools.

item.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
item.description: AI Revolution: AI bots now outnumber humans in web requests, Google AI Overviews reduce user clicks on links, and AI agents are beginning to browse and transact online. The video argues this represents a fundamental shift in how the internet is used, with machines increasingly mediating human access to web content.

item.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI demonstrates how to build a custom Claude Code agentic OS using Fable 5, covering architecture setup, memory, observability, and skills customization. The approach enables developers to increase Claude Code efficiency through a structured three-step process.

item.title: Stop Prop Drilling Your React Modals (Use react-call)
item.description: Better Stack: react-call is a lightweight utility that enables calling React components as asynchronous functions, eliminating prop drilling and state management boilerplate. It uses Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow architectures to handle layout isolation and loading states in a linear pattern.

item.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
item.description: Austin Marchese: Austin Marchese presents six "power phrases" claimed to improve Claude Code productivity by 10x. The video emphasizes that gains come not from better prompts, smarter models, or plugins, but from specific phrasing patterns endorsed by Claude Code's creator and others like Andrej Karpathy, plus a cautionary phrase many users misuse.

item.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates Codex's /goal command, which initiates a loop called Ralph that autonomously plans, executes, and tests code steps without user intervention. Users describe the desired outcome in plain English and the system self-corrects if errors occur.

item.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology explains how AI agents are reshaping software design by leveraging design systems and context engineering. The video discusses how MCP (Model Context Protocol) and AI agents work together to build consistent and correct applications.

item.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
item.description: Jack Roberts: This tutorial demonstrates how to build a self-improving knowledge base for the Hermes AI agent using Obsidian as a wiki vault, integrating it with Claude and NotebookLM. The workflow automatically ingests meeting notes, emails, and research through cron jobs powered by Granola, implementing Karpathy's LLM wiki principles to overcome LLM context limitations.

item.title: OpenRouter Fusion: They OFFICIALLY CLAIM THAT THIS MODEL beats FABLE?
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing reviews OpenRouter's Fusion API, which combines multiple models through a judge to claim Fable-level performance at half the cost. Testing reveals the marketing claims are misleading: while Fusion performs well on Deep Research benchmarks, it underperforms on practical tasks like coding, math, and SVG generation, often costing more and responding slower than single strong models.

item.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search covers a weekly digest of AI news including model releases (GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3), research projects in vision and avatars (Flex4DHuman, MeshFlow, Millivid), new Google models and features (Gemini Live Translate, DiffusionGemma), and text-to-speech tools. The video notes Claude Fable's discontinuation and highlights open-source AI developments across multiple domains.

item.title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
item.description: Better Stack: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving 3,000+ tokens per second on standard hardware. The architecture combines selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime to eliminate inference bottlenecks.

item.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and characterizes this as marking the start of a permanent underclass. The video appears to discuss consequences of AI policy decisions.

item.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down after three days following a US government order restricting public access to frontier AI systems. The video argues this raises critical questions about whether powerful AI models will remain accessible to the general public or become restricted to government control.

item.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube commentary claiming that OpenAI's market dominance is eroding due to delays in GPT-6 release, while competitors Microsoft, Apple, and Google have shifted to alternative models. The video discusses GPT-6 rumors, benchmark performance across models including Claude and Gemini, and broader industry consolidation.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin discusses the sudden withdrawal of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model following a US government export-control directive allegedly triggered by a claimed jailbreak. The video covers the regulatory decision, performance benchmarks Medin conducted before the model was pulled, and implications for developers building on LLMs.

item.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
item.description: Better Stack: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a text generation model that applies image-generation diffusion techniques to text using uniform state diffusion for iterative refinement. The model achieves theoretical speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs by shifting from memory-bound to compute-bound execution through bidirectional attention and multi-pass token correction.

item.title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
item.description: Jack Roberts: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model that uses Sparse Attention to process documents 15.6x faster than comparable models while reducing compute costs to one-twentieth of the previous generation. It achieves efficiency by selectively focusing on relevant parts of input rather than processing everything.

item.title: GLM-5.2 (Fully Tested): I got EARLY ACCESS & This MODEL is CRAZY!
item.description: AICodeKing: GLM-5.2 is a new language model from Z AI featuring a 1 million token context window, expected to be open-weight and MIT licensed, and accessible through the GLM Coding Plan subscription at approximately $8 per month. The model scores 81.43 on coding benchmarks, approximately 6 percent below Opus 4.8 and Fable, with strong performance on code generation and local fine-tuning tasks.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

item.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
item.description: Better Stack: The US government has issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers immediately. The report cites this as a breaking regulatory action affecting model availability.

item.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
item.description: AI Revolution: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a different approach and runs up to four times faster on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and self-correction capabilities.

item.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It offers multiple interfaces including a desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK for flexible agentic workflows.

item.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
item.description: Google for Developers: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler game built on Angular and Phaser.js, powered by Gemma 4, that teaches developers about vibe coding and agentic behaviors. It demonstrates NPC interactions where the model generates code and executes autonomous thinking loops with tool calls. Gemma 4 can be served locally via Transformers.js, Ollama, or LM Studio, or routed through the Gemini API and Google Cloud.

item.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej teaches a course on Pi Agent, a framework for building real coding agents. The video covers Pi Agent fundamentals in a condensed format and links to a full course on his website.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates Claude Fable 5's website design capabilities through progressive challenges, comparing it against Claude Opus 4.8. The video argues that Fable 5 represents a significant shift in AI-driven web design through superior prompt handling, token efficiency, and design extraction methods.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music playlist or streaming service created and curated by musicians. It is designed to provide ambient music for thinking and building activities.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service curated for thinking and building, made by musicians. It provides ambient background music designed to support focused work and creative activity.

item.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
item.description: AI Master: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that automates multi-format marketing campaigns by generating TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts from a single prompt. It maintains character and style consistency across content formats and eliminates manual shoot-edit workflows.

item.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates using Gemini to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL. Gemini provides both code translation and explanations for data type conversions like IDENTITY and BIT, helping developers learn PostgreSQL through guided examples.

item.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
item.description: Better Stack: CopilotKit is a framework that helps developers move beyond bolted-on chatbots to agent-native applications. It enables AI agents to render real UI components, share application state with React/Next.js apps, and support human-in-the-loop approval workflows for AI-driven features.

item.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery demonstrates techniques for implementing persistent memory in AI agents. The video covers approaches to enable agents to retain and recall information across interactions.

item.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
item.description: EO: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data to help teams make better decisions. Founded by Eléonore Crespo, it has raised over $400 million and serves major customers like Figma, Brex, and Anthropic, growing 2x year over year.

item.title: Don’t miss this Claude update
item.description: Jack Roberts: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which enables launching hundreds of subagents simultaneously within a single session to parallelize task execution. The system splits complex work across multiple agents, with half performing tasks and half verifying, designed for large codebases, multi-file research, and work that would normally take days.

item.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
item.description: IBM Technology: The Mixture of Experts podcast hosted by Tim Hwang and guests debates Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 release and its tier routing system, Apple's WWDC 2026 announcement to partner with NVIDIA for cloud AI instead of on-device models, and the capability of AI models to detect sarcasm.

item.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

item.title: Claude Code (Fable) V/S Codex (GPT-5.5) : MATHEMATICAL TEST = ONE CLEAR WINNER!
item.description: AICodeKing: A comparison of AI subscription plans (ChatGPT Plus, ChatGPT Pro, Claude Pro, Claude Max) versus direct API pricing. The analysis argues that subscription plans offer significantly higher value-to-cost ratios for heavy users, with Claude Pro providing around $400 of API-equivalent usage for $20/month and ChatGPT Pro offering up to $14,000 equivalent value at $200/month, though only worthwhile if usage limits are fully utilized.

item.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

item.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
item.description: Claude: Anthropic held an opening keynote at Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo featuring updates from engineering and product leaders. The event appears to be a developer-focused conference announcement.

item.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
item.description: AI Revolution: Claude Fable 5 faces backlash for refusing harmless prompts, triggering false safety alarms, and limiting advanced AI work without clear disclosure. The controversy centers on whether users can trust the actual model output versus hidden guardrails or fallback behavior.

item.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

item.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
item.description: Google for Developers: Yossi Matias, VP and Head of Google Research, discusses the "magic cycle" of research from breakthrough to real-world impact at Google scale. The conversation covers AI applications in flood forecasting, MedGemma, and Gemini for Science, along with emerging techniques and tools like speculative decoding, generative UI, and the Willow quantum chip.

item.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud tutorial on building AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Looker. Covers the Model Context Protocol (MCP), MCP Toolbox for Databases, tool configuration, API credential setup, and grounding agents in enterprise data through a Python environment.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates a "ZeroEdit" pipeline for building YouTube channels by using Claude Code (Fable 5) to automate video editing, Higsfield CLI for visual composition, Auphonic for audio processing, and reverse-engineered channel templates to produce faceless YouTube videos at roughly $2 per video. The approach involves script generation, voice recording, and automated visual-to-audio matching.

item.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: Studio is an internal workspace that lets users ask natural language questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Reliability features include preflight sequencing to manage context windows, layering rules to prioritize primary sources, and query validation before widget hardcoding.

item.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
item.description: Cursor: Sam Whitmore from Cursor discusses scaling coding agents with Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge from Baseten. The conversation explores running agents at scale, enabling inter-agent communication and review, and designing systems rather than managing parallel tasks.

item.title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, argues that smart glasses could become the next major computing platform by leveraging on-device AI assistants for daily tasks, context-aware help, and real-time information without disrupting user attention. The discussion covers Meta's strategy with Ray-Ban glasses, competitive pressures from OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and privacy concerns around facial recognition and device-side processing.

item.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
item.description: Jordan Urbs: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.
